LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION FAIL BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A PRIMA FACIE CASE. FURTHER, DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF AN APPROPRIATE REASON FOR PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE THAT THE REASON FOR THE TERMINATION WAS A PRETEXT AND THAT DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS MOTIVATED DEFENDANT. THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION FAIL BECAUSE THE CLAIMS ARE DUPLICATIVE OF THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION AND ARE BARRED BY THE FAILURE TO FILE A GOVERNMENT TORT CLAIM AND ARE SUBJECT TO THE WORKERS COMPENSATION EXCLUSIVE REMEDY. DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER IS CONSISTENT WITH THE COURT'S FINDINGS. COURT OVERRULED OBJECTION TO WINNIE INCIDENT/LETTER. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-24-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Frederick C. Roesti, Esq. of Law Office of Frederick C. Roesti 571-0313 for pltf & opposing party; Michael J. Leon Guerrero, Deputy City Atty 554-4285 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Plaintiff's 1st and 2nd causes of action for discrimination fail because plaintiff has not presented evidence of a prima facie case. Further, defendant has presented evidence of an appropriate reason for plaintiff's termination. Plaintiff has presented no evidence that the reason for the termination was a pretext and that discriminatory animus motivated defendant. The 3rd and 4th causes of action fail because the claims are duplicative of the 1st and 2nd causes of action and are barred by the failure to file a government tort claim and are subject to the workers compensation exclusive remedy. Defendant's proposed order is consistent with the Court's findings. Court overruled objection to Winnie incident/letter. Order and judgment signed in open court. (302/PJM/ER) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
